title: santalodes humile
intro: Santalodes humile, also known as Indian sandalwood or East Indian sandalwood, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant wood and aromatic properties. Traditionally, the plant's bark and roots have been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Santalodes humile include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Santalodes humile is often used in essential oils, teas, and powders, and is also used in traditional perfumes and incense sticks.
